entry.title: Transitivity of the Subformula Relation for Propositional Logic

link.title: Transitivity of the Subformula Relation for Propositional Logic

entry.content:
Problem: Show that the relation “is a subformula of” is transitive.

Definition 2.1.7 The set of subformulas \(Sub(\phi)\) is given by

\[\begin{aligned} Sub(\phi) &= \{ \phi \} \text{ for atomic } \phi \\ Sub(\phi_1 * \phi_2) &= Sub(\phi_1) \cup Sub(\phi_2) \cup \{\phi_1 * \phi_2\} \\ Sub(\neg \phi) &= Sub(\phi) \cup \{\neg \phi\} \\ \end{aligned}\]
entry.summary: Exercise 2.1.3 from Dalen's "Logic and Structure", 5th edition.
